steps.title: Match the comparison
steps.content: “Compared with that of a highly concentrated market” uses “that” to mean the HHI score. The subject after the comma must therefore be another HHI score, not the market itself.

steps.title: Keep the subjects parallel
steps.content: Choice A and Choice D compare a score with a market, creating a dangling comparison. Choice C turns the second side into a conditional clause and still fails to put the score in parallel position. The version beginning with “the HHI score of a less concentrated market” makes the comparison grammatical. The answer is B.
